assertions.text: Output begins with an approximate word count and estimated runtime
Script opens with a labeled hook section covering the first 30 seconds of spoken content
Each section is marked with a bold header matching the talking points provided in the input
On-camera direction notes appear in [square brackets] at least twice in the script
Script ends with a specific call-to-action that is placed naturally within the outro
Estimated runtime is 60 seconds or less
Output contains two clearly labeled parts: Shorts Script and On-Screen Text Suggestions
On-Screen Text Suggestions include 3-5 specific text overlays with timestamps
The script is self-contained and understandable without having seen the source video
No transitional long-form language such as 'as I mentioned earlier' or 'in the next section' appears
